violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation brown residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC informed to sanitize in 3 bay sink until dishwasher is serviced and dispensing proper concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
Meatballs and chili which was prepared on site, date marked 9/15 & 9/22 was observed held beyond the 7 days. Food items exceeding the 7 days shall be discarded. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

(Windex) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

The door to the outside, located at the 1st & 2nd floor, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection (staff failed to supply inspector with proof of certification). New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher in the bar area was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

Working containers of dry ingredients are incorrectly labeled or not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

The following in-use utensils were observed improperly stored or used: 1) Cups (with no handles) are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients 2) Ice scoop in main level bar was observed stored with handle in direct contact with ice Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) The base of the faucet of the kitchen prep sink had an active leak 2) The cold water handle of the three bay sink faucet in the third level bar was not dispensing water 3) The cold water handle of the hand sink in the second (main) level bar was not dispensing water 4) Several lids of dry ingredient containers were cracked (piece of lid observed fallen into dry ingredients) New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The condensate from the ice well unit in the third level bar is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024
